name: planner
content:
**Plan:**

- Step 1:
      * sub-task: DNS Simulation — copy template, modify production loop, run full simulation
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Copy `/home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/dns_template_1024.py` to `codebase/step_1.py`
        - Modify only the production loop: replace the short test block with a full T_prod = 50 × T_L production run
        - Use forcing at k ∈ [3, 5], Δt_snap = 0.05, Δt_vel = 2.0, N_tracers = 5000, N = 1024
        - During spinup (T/T_L ≥ 10), save diagnostics only; seed tracers at start of production
        - Save all required output files to `/home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/data/`: tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, velocity_snapshots.npy, vorticity_snapshots.npy, vel_times.npy, energy_spectrum.npy, diagnostics.npy, sim_params.json
        - Coarsen Eulerian fields 1024→256 using average pooling before saving
        - Print key diagnostics: T_L at end of spinup, T_prod, N_tracer_snaps, N_vel_snaps, U_rms, k_peak at end
      * code_execution_timeout: 7200

- Step 2:
      * sub-task: Data quality validation and inverse cascade confirmation
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, diagnostics.npy, energy_spectrum.npy from the data directory
        - Compute mean tracer displacement per snapshot step; verify it is < 0.3 × 2π ≈ 1.88; print result and flag if it fails
        - Compute VACF C_vv(τ) at lags τ = 1, 2, 4, 8, 16 steps; verify C_vv(τ=1) > 0.3; report decorrelation time τ_corr
        - Plot E(k) at t = 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 1.0 × T_prod on a single figure with 4 panels or overlaid curves; confirm k_peak drifts from ~4 toward ~1–2
        - Save the quality validation plot and print all key numerical results
      * code_execution_timeout: 600

- Step 3:
      * sub-task: Non-stationarity analysis — compute α(t), γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t) in overlapping windows
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, diagnostics.npy from the data directory
        - Define overlapping windows of width W = 8 T_L, step ΔW = 2 T_L; extract ~26 windows
        - For each window: fit Lévy-stable α via McCulloch quantile method at lags τ = {0.5, 1, 2} × T_L using x and y displacements separately; also compute Hill estimator as cross-check
        - For each window: fit MSD(τ) power law to get γ(t); fit VACF algebraic decay to get ν(t)
        - Extract d_vv(t) and k_peak(t) from diagnostics for each window center
        - Run ADF and KPSS stationarity tests on α(t), γ(t), ν(t) time series
        - Save all window results (α, γ, ν, d_vv, k_peak, window center times) to a numpy/npz file for use in later steps
        - Print all numerical results including stationarity test p-values
      * code_execution_timeout: 1800

- Step 4:
      * sub-task: Wavelet decomposition, eddy-lifetime estimation, and filtered tracer re-advection
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load velocity_snapshots.npy (shape N_vel_snaps × 2 × 256 × 256), vel_times.npy, tracer_positions.npy, tracer_times.npy from the data directory
        - Apply 2D DWT (Daubechies db4, periodic boundary mode) to each velocity snapshot at levels j = 1 to 6
        - Reconstruct filtered velocity fields ũ_j for j_min ∈ {1, 2, 3, 4} by zeroing detail coefficients below j_min
        - For each scale j, compute wavelet energy E_j(x,y,t) = sum of squared detail coefficients; compute temporal autocorrelation C_E(τ,j) averaged over spatial positions; integrate to get T_eddy(j) (trapezoidal, stop when C_E < 0.1)
        - Re-advect the saved tracer initial positions in each filtered field ũ_j using RK4 integration with bilinear interpolation; use the same tracer seeds as the main simulation
        - For each filtered field: compute α(j) via McCulloch method, γ_MSD(j) from MSD power law, ν_VACF(j) from VACF decay
        - Save all wavelet results (T_eddy per scale, α(j), γ(j), ν(j), filtered tracer trajectories summary) to npz file
        - Print all numerical results
      * code_execution_timeout: 7200

- Step 5:
      * sub-task: LCS identification via Okubo-Weiss, FTLE computation, residence/flight time analysis
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load velocity_snapshots.npy, vel_times.npy, tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, diagnostics.npy from the data directory
        - Compute Okubo-Weiss parameter Q = s² − ω² from velocity snapshots; use dynamic threshold Q_threshold(t) = σ_Q(t)
        - Classify each tracer at each time as vortex-trapped (Q < −Q_threshold), strain-dominated (Q > +Q_threshold), or neutral via bilinear interpolation of Q field at tracer positions
        - Compute α separately for vortex-trapped and strain-dominated tracer populations using McCulloch method
        - Compute FTLE on 256×256 grid with integration time ΔT_FTLE = 2 × T_L using bilinear interpolation between velocity snapshots
        - Extract residence time distributions and flight displacement distributions from trajectory segments classified by Q; test for power-law tails; compare α from CCDF slope to McCulloch α(t)
        - Distinguish vortex-death vs strain-stripping ejection events; compute fractions
        - Save all LCS/FTLE results to npz file; print key statistics
      * code_execution_timeout: 3600

- Step 6:
      * sub-task: Causal link analysis — cross-correlation and Granger causality between spectral condensation and α(t)
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load the window results from Step 3 (α(t), γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t)) and diagnostics
        - Compute time-lagged cross-correlation between dE(k=1)/dt and dα(t)/dt at lags 1–3 windows
        - Run Granger causality test (statsmodels VAR or grangercausalitytests) with lag = 1–3 windows on the α(t) and k_peak(t) series
        - Compute spatial density of hyperbolic points (Q > +Q_threshold) from LCS results; correlate with α(t)
        - Save causal analysis results to npz/json file; print all test statistics and p-values
      * code_execution_timeout: 600

- Step 7:
      * sub-task: Generate all plots — quality validation, non-stationarity, wavelet scaling, LCS, synthesis
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load all saved results from Steps 2–6 (window results, wavelet results, LCS results, causal results, energy spectra, diagnostics)
        - Plot 1: E(k) at 4 time fractions of T_prod showing inverse cascade development (k_peak drift)
        - Plot 2: VACF C_vv(τ) vs lag steps; mark τ_corr
        - Plot 3: α(t), γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t) vs time in overlapping windows with McCulloch and Hill α overlaid; mark ADF/KPSS stationarity results
        - Plot 4: α(t) vs k_peak(t)/k_box scatter with fitted curve α = a + b × (k_peak/k_box)^c
        - Plot 5: α(j) vs T_eddy(j) for wavelet scales j = 1–4 with CTRW prediction curve; also show γ(j) and ν(j) vs T_eddy(j)
        - Plot 6: Vortex-trapped vs strain-dominated α comparison; residence time and flight displacement CCDF log-log plots
        - Plot 7: FTLE field snapshot at representative time; overlay tracer classification
        - Plot 8: 2D map of α as function of (T_eddy, k_peak/k_box); cross-correlation and Granger causality summary
        - Save all plots as high-resolution PNG files; print file paths
      * code_execution_timeout: 600

- Step 8:
      * sub-task: Write full Results section of the paper
      * agent: researcher
      * bullet points:
        - Synthesize all numerical results from Steps 2–7: displacement quality check, VACF decorrelation, inverse cascade confirmation, α(t)/γ(t)/ν(t) time series, stationarity tests, wavelet eddy-lifetime scaling, LCS classification, FTLE, causal analysis
        - Report whether α ≈ 1.4 from v1 is stationary or drifts with spectral condensation
        - Report the functional form α = f(T_eddy) from wavelet analysis and whether CTRW prediction α = 1 + β/(2−β) with β ∝ T_eddy^δ holds
        - Discuss vortex-trapped vs strain-dominated α values and ejection fractions
        - Discuss Granger causality and cross-correlation results linking spectral condensation to α(t) evolution
        - Write a complete, publication-quality Results section with subsections matching the analysis tasks; reference all plots by figure number; include all key quantitative values with uncertainties

name: plan_reviewer
content:
**Recommendations:**

- Step 1 instructs the engineer to use forcing at k ∈ [3, 5], but the template file at `/home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/dns_template_1024.py` uses k ∈ [1, 3] according to the plan's own Section 0. The instruction must explicitly state that the engineer should change `k_force_min` and `k_force_max` to 3 and 5 respectively, and also update `nu_h` from the template value (3.9e-31) to the problem specification value (1e-19) with p=4, since these are critical physical parameters that differ between the template and the problem specification.

- Step 1 does not specify what to do if the 7200-second timeout is insufficient to complete the full T_prod = 50 × T_L run. Given that T_L can range from 3 to 15 simulation units and T_prod can therefore span 150–750 simulation units at N=1024 on GPU, the instruction should add a checkpoint-saving mechanism: periodically flush accumulated tracer/velocity arrays to disk (e.g., every 500 tracer snapshots) so that partial results are not lost if the run is interrupted, and the simulation can resume from the last checkpoint.

- Step 2 should explicitly instruct the engineer to handle the periodic boundary condition when computing tracer displacements: the displacement Δr = r(t+Δt) − r(t) must be wrapped to [−π, π] before computing the magnitude, otherwise tracers crossing the periodic boundary will appear to have artificially large displacements of order 2π, which would falsely fail the quality check.

- Step 3 should specify that the McCulloch quantile method must be applied to the displacement increments at each lag (not cumulative displacements from t=0), and that x and y components should be pooled together after verifying isotropy, with the median α across lags and components reported as the window estimate along with its standard deviation across lags as an uncertainty measure.

- Step 3 should instruct the engineer to also save the per-window MSD curves and VACF curves (not just the fitted exponents γ and ν) to the npz file, so that Step 7 can plot representative MSD and VACF curves for selected windows to visually validate the power-law fits.

- Step 4 is very computationally heavy (DWT decomposition of all velocity snapshots, re-advection of 5000 tracers in 4 filtered fields, and statistical fitting) and may not complete within 7200 seconds. The instruction should specify that the re-advection in filtered fields should use a coarser time step (e.g., Δt_adv = Δt_vel = 2.0, interpolating between saved velocity snapshots) rather than re-running the full DNS, and that only the first 500 tracer snapshots worth of time should be used for the filtered re-advection to keep computation tractable.

- Step 5 combines four distinct heavy computations (Okubo-Weiss classification, FTLE computation, residence/flight time analysis, and ejection event classification) into a single step with a 3600-second timeout. The instruction should prioritize: first complete the Okubo-Weiss classification and α computation for vortex-trapped vs strain-dominated populations (highest scientific priority), then FTLE if time permits, and explicitly state that FTLE computation should be limited to 3 representative time snapshots rather than the full time series to stay within the timeout.

- Step 6 should explicitly instruct the engineer to verify that the α(t) and k_peak(t) time series are long enough (at least 15–20 data points) for Granger causality to be statistically meaningful, and to report the effective degrees of freedom. If fewer than 15 windows are available, the Granger test should be skipped and only the cross-correlation reported.

- Step 7 should merge Plot 1 and Plot 2 into a single multi-panel figure (E(k) cascade + VACF), and merge Plot 6 and Plot 7 into another multi-panel figure (LCS classification + FTLE + CCDF), reducing the total number of figures from 8 to 6. This keeps the plotting step within the 600-second timeout and produces cleaner publication figures. Each merged figure should be saved as a single PNG file.

- Step 8 should instruct the researcher to explicitly address the discrepancy between the CTRW prediction α = 1 + β/(2−β) and the measured α(j) values from wavelet-filtered advection, quantifying the residual and discussing whether the deviation is within estimator uncertainty or indicates a genuine breakdown of the CTRW framework. The researcher should also compare the non-stationarity findings against the prior v1 result of α ≈ 1.4 and state whether the new long-run results confirm, refine, or contradict that estimate.

name: reviewer_response_evaluator
content:
**Recommendations:**

Step 1 must explicitly instruct the engineer to update k_force_min to 3, k_force_max to 5, and nu_h to 1e-19 with p=4 to match the problem specification, overriding the template defaults.

Step 1 must include a checkpoint-saving mechanism to periodically flush tracer and velocity arrays to disk to prevent data loss during the 7200-second timeout.

Step 2 must instruct the engineer to wrap tracer displacements Δr to [-π, π] to correctly handle periodic boundary conditions.

Step 3 must specify that the McCulloch quantile method be applied to displacement increments at each lag, pooling x and y components after verifying isotropy, and reporting the median α and standard deviation across lags.

Step 3 must instruct the engineer to save per-window MSD and VACF curves to the npz file for visual validation of power-law fits.

Step 4 must specify using a coarser time step (Δt_adv = 2.0) for re-advection in filtered fields and limit the analysis to the first 500 tracer snapshots to ensure completion within the timeout.

Step 5 must prioritize Okubo-Weiss classification and α computation, and limit FTLE computation to 3 representative time snapshots to stay within the 3600-second timeout.

Step 6 must instruct the engineer to verify that α(t) and k_peak(t) time series contain at least 15 data points for Granger causality, otherwise defaulting to cross-correlation.

Step 7 must merge plots into multi-panel figures (E(k) cascade + VACF and LCS classification + FTLE + CCDF) to reduce the total figure count to 6 and ensure completion within the 600-second timeout.

Step 8 must instruct the researcher to quantify the residual between the CTRW prediction and measured α(j) values and compare the results against the prior v1 estimate of α ≈ 1.4.

name: review_recorder
content:
Recommendations have been logged.
Number of feedback rounds left: 0.
Now, update the plan accordingly, planner!

name: planner
content:
**Plan:**

- Step 1:
      * sub-task: DNS Simulation — copy template, override physical parameters, run full simulation with checkpointing
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Copy `/home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/dns_template_1024.py` to `codebase/step_1.py`
        - Override template defaults: set k_force_min=3, k_force_max=5, nu_h=1e-19, p=4 (hyperviscosity order)
        - Modify only the production loop: replace the short test block with a full T_prod = 50 × T_L production run using Δt_snap=0.05, Δt_vel=2.0, N_tracers=5000, N=1024
        - During spinup (T/T_L ≥ 10), save diagnostics only; seed 5000 tracers uniformly at random at the start of production
        - Implement checkpoint-saving: every 500 tracer snapshots, flush accumulated tracer_positions, tracer_velocities, tracer_times, velocity_snapshots, vorticity_snapshots, vel_times, energy_spectrum, diagnostics arrays to disk so partial results survive timeout
        - Coarsen Eulerian fields 1024→256 using average pooling (factor 4) before saving velocity_snapshots and vorticity_snapshots
        - Save all required output files to `/home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/data/` with the exact filenames and shapes specified in the problem
        - Save sim_params.json with all key parameters including T_L at end of spinup, T_prod, force parameters, dt_snap, dt_vel
        - Print key diagnostics at end: T_L at spinup end, T_prod, N_tracer_snaps, N_vel_snaps, U_rms, k_peak
      * code_execution_timeout: 7200

- Step 2:
      * sub-task: Data quality validation and inverse cascade confirmation
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, diagnostics.npy, energy_spectrum.npy from the data directory
        - Compute mean tracer displacement per snapshot step; wrap Δr to [−π, π] for each component before computing magnitude to handle periodic boundary crossings correctly; verify mean displacement < 0.3 × 2π ≈ 1.88; print result and flag failure if exceeded
        - Compute VACF C_vv(τ) at lags τ = 1, 2, 4, 8, 16 steps using tracer_velocities; verify C_vv(τ=1) > 0.3; report decorrelation time τ_corr as the first lag where C_vv drops below 1/e
        - Plot E(k) at t = 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 1.0 × T_prod overlaid on a single panel with log-log axes; mark k_peak at each time; confirm k_peak drifts from ~4 toward ~1–2
        - Combine E(k) cascade plot and VACF plot into a single multi-panel figure; save as high-resolution PNG
        - Print all key numerical results: mean displacement, C_vv at each lag, τ_corr, k_peak at each time fraction
      * code_execution_timeout: 600

- Step 3:
      * sub-task: Non-stationarity analysis — compute α(t), γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t) in overlapping windows
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, diagnostics.npy from the data directory; load sim_params.json to get T_L
        - Define overlapping windows of width W = 8 T_L, step ΔW = 2 T_L; extract up to ~26 windows
        - For each window: compute displacement increments at lags τ = {0.5, 1, 2} × T_L (not cumulative from t=0); verify isotropy by comparing x and y displacement distributions; pool x and y components; apply McCulloch quantile method to get α per lag; report median α across lags as window estimate and standard deviation across lags as uncertainty
        - For each window: also compute Hill estimator on the pooled displacement magnitudes as cross-check
        - For each window: compute MSD(τ) at multiple lags and fit power law to get γ(t); compute VACF and fit algebraic decay to get ν(t); save the full MSD and VACF curves (not just exponents) for later plotting
        - Extract d_vv(t) and k_peak(t) from diagnostics averaged over each window
        - Run ADF and KPSS stationarity tests on α(t), γ(t), ν(t) time series; print all test statistics and p-values
        - Fit α = a + b × (k_peak/k_box)^c to the α(t) vs k_peak(t)/k_box data; print fit parameters
        - Save all window results including per-window MSD curves, VACF curves, α_McCulloch, α_Hill, γ, ν, d_vv, k_peak, window center times to a single npz file
        - Print all numerical results
      * code_execution_timeout: 1800

- Step 4:
      * sub-task: Wavelet decomposition, eddy-lifetime estimation, and filtered tracer re-advection
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load velocity_snapshots.npy (shape N_vel_snaps × 2 × 256 × 256), vel_times.npy, tracer_positions.npy, tracer_times.npy from the data directory
        - Apply 2D DWT (Daubechies db4, periodic boundary mode) to each velocity snapshot at levels j = 1 to 6; store detail coefficients at each level
        - Reconstruct filtered velocity fields ũ_j for j_min ∈ {1, 2, 3, 4} by zeroing detail coefficients below j_min; store reconstructed fields
        - For each scale j, compute wavelet energy E_j(x,y,t) = sum of squared detail coefficients across H/V/D subbands; compute temporal autocorrelation C_E(τ,j) averaged over all spatial positions; integrate trapezoidal to get T_eddy(j), stopping when C_E drops below 0.1
        - Re-advect tracer initial positions in each filtered field ũ_j using RK4 with bilinear interpolation between saved velocity snapshots; use Δt_adv = 2.0 (matching Δt_vel) and limit to the first 500 tracer snapshots worth of time to stay within timeout
        - For each filtered field: compute α(j) via McCulloch method on displacement increments, γ_MSD(j) from MSD power law fit, ν_VACF(j) from VACF algebraic decay fit
        - Fit α(j) vs T_eddy(j) to extract functional form; test CTRW prediction α = 1 + β/(2−β) with β ∝ T_eddy^δ by fitting δ
        - Save all wavelet results (T_eddy per scale, α(j), γ(j), ν(j), C_E curves, fit parameters) to npz file
        - Print all numerical results including T_eddy values, α(j), γ(j), ν(j), δ, and CTRW residuals
      * code_execution_timeout: 7200

- Step 5:
      * sub-task: LCS identification via Okubo-Weiss, FTLE computation, residence/flight time analysis
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load velocity_snapshots.npy, vel_times.npy, tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, diagnostics.npy from the data directory
        - Priority 1: Compute Okubo-Weiss parameter Q = s² − ω² from velocity snapshots using finite differences; use dynamic threshold Q_threshold(t) = σ_Q(t); classify each tracer at each time as vortex-trapped (Q < −Q_threshold), strain-dominated (Q > +Q_threshold), or neutral via bilinear interpolation of Q field at tracer positions
        - Priority 2: Compute α separately for vortex-trapped and strain-dominated tracer populations using McCulloch method on displacement increments; print α values for each population
        - Priority 3: Extract residence time distributions and flight displacement distributions from trajectory segments classified by Q; test for power-law tails via CCDF log-log fit; compare α from CCDF slope to McCulloch α; distinguish vortex-death vs strain-stripping ejection events and compute fractions
        - Priority 4 (if time permits): Compute FTLE on 256×256 grid at exactly 3 representative time snapshots (t = 0.25, 0.5, 0.75 × T_prod) with integration time ΔT_FTLE = 2 × T_L using bilinear interpolation between velocity snapshots
        - Save all LCS/FTLE results to npz file including Q fields, tracer classifications, α per population, residence/flight distributions, ejection fractions, and FTLE fields if computed
        - Print key statistics: α_vortex, α_strain, ejection fractions, power-law fit results
      * code_execution_timeout: 3600

- Step 6:
      * sub-task: Causal link analysis — cross-correlation and Granger causality between spectral condensation and α(t)
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load window results npz from Step 3 (α_McCulloch(t), k_peak(t), window center times) and diagnostics; load energy_spectrum.npy and tracer_times.npy
        - Verify that α(t) and k_peak(t) time series contain at least 15 data points; if fewer than 15 windows are available, skip Granger causality and report only cross-correlation
        - Compute time-lagged cross-correlation between dE(k=1)/dt and dα(t)/dt at lags 0, 1, 2, 3 windows; report peak lag and correlation coefficient
        - If ≥ 15 windows: run Granger causality test using statsmodels grangercausalitytests with max lag = 3 on the (α(t), k_peak(t)) bivariate series; report F-statistics and p-values for each lag
        - Load LCS results from Step 5; compute spatial density of hyperbolic points (Q > +Q_threshold) per time snapshot; interpolate to window centers; compute Pearson correlation between hyperbolic point density and α(t)
        - Save all causal analysis results (cross-correlation arrays, Granger test results, hyperbolic density correlation) to npz/json file
        - Print all test statistics, p-values, and effective degrees of freedom
      * code_execution_timeout: 600

- Step 7:
      * sub-task: Generate all plots — merged multi-panel figures covering all analysis results
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load all saved results from Steps 2–6 (window results npz, wavelet results npz, LCS results npz, causal results, energy spectra, diagnostics, sim_params.json)
        - Figure 1 (multi-panel): E(k) cascade at 4 time fractions + VACF C_vv(τ) vs lag; mark k_peak drift and τ_corr
        - Figure 2 (multi-panel): α(t) with McCulloch and Hill overlaid, γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t) vs window center time; annotate ADF/KPSS stationarity results as text on panels
        - Figure 3 (multi-panel): α(t) vs k_peak(t)/k_box scatter with fitted curve α = a + b × (k_peak/k_box)^c; representative MSD curves for 3 selected windows; representative VACF curves for 3 selected windows
        - Figure 4 (multi-panel): α(j) vs T_eddy(j) with CTRW prediction curve; γ(j) vs T_eddy(j); ν(j) vs T_eddy(j); wavelet energy autocorrelation C_E(τ,j) for each scale
        - Figure 5 (multi-panel): LCS classification + FTLE field snapshot at representative time with tracer overlay; vortex-trapped vs strain-dominated α comparison bar chart; residence time CCDF and flight displacement CCDF log-log plots
        - Figure 6 (multi-panel): Cross-correlation between dE(k=1)/dt and dα/dt vs lag; Granger causality p-values vs lag; 2D map of α as function of (T_eddy, k_peak/k_box)
        - Save all 6 figures as high-resolution PNG files (dpi=300); print file paths
      * code_execution_timeout: 600

- Step 8:
      * sub-task: Write full Results section of the paper
      * agent: researcher
      * bullet points:
        - Synthesize all numerical results from Steps 2–7 into a complete, publication-quality Results section with subsections matching the four analysis tasks
        - Subsection 1 (Data Quality and Inverse Cascade): report mean displacement, C_vv(τ=1), τ_corr, k_peak drift from ~4 to ~1–2 over T_prod
        - Subsection 2 (Non-Stationarity Analysis): report α(t) time series with McCulloch and Hill estimates, γ(t), ν(t), ADF/KPSS test results, fit parameters for α = a + b × (k_peak/k_box)^c; state whether α is stationary or drifts; compare to prior v1 estimate of α ≈ 1.4
        - Subsection 3 (Wavelet Eddy-Lifetime Scaling): report T_eddy(j) for each scale, α(j), γ(j), ν(j); report functional form α = f(T_eddy); test CTRW prediction α = 1 + β/(2−β) with β ∝ T_eddy^δ; quantify residual between CTRW prediction and measured α(j) and discuss whether deviation is within estimator uncertainty or indicates genuine CTRW breakdown
        - Subsection 4 (LCS and Ejection Dynamics): report α_vortex vs α_strain, ejection fractions (vortex-death vs strain-stripping), power-law exponents from residence time and flight displacement CCDFs, FTLE ridge structure
        - Subsection 5 (Causal Analysis): report cross-correlation peak lag and coefficient, Granger causality F-statistics and p-values, hyperbolic point density correlation with α(t); state whether spectral condensation Granger-causes changes in α
        - Reference all figures by number; include all key quantitative values with uncertainties; write in third-person academic style

name: planner_response_evaluator
content:
**PLAN**

- Step 1:
	* sub-task: DNS Simulation — copy template, override physical parameters, run full simulation with checkpointing
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* code_execution_timeout: 7200s

	* instructions:
		- Copy /home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/dns_template_1024.py to codebase/step_1.py
		- Override template defaults: set k_force_min=3, k_force_max=5, nu_h=1e-19, p=4 (hyperviscosity order)
		- Modify only the production loop: replace the short test block with a full T_prod = 50 × T_L production run using Δt_snap=0.05, Δt_vel=2.0, N_tracers=5000, N=1024
		- During spinup (T/T_L ≥ 10), save diagnostics only; seed 5000 tracers uniformly at random at the start of production
		- Implement checkpoint-saving: every 500 tracer snapshots, flush accumulated tracer_positions, tracer_velocities, tracer_times, velocity_snapshots, vorticity_snapshots, vel_times, energy_spectrum, diagnostics arrays to disk so partial results survive timeout
		- Coarsen Eulerian fields 1024→256 using average pooling (factor 4) before saving velocity_snapshots and vorticity_snapshots
		- Save all required output files to /home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/data/ with the exact filenames and shapes specified in the problem
		- Save sim_params.json with all key parameters including T_L at end of spinup, T_prod, force parameters, dt_snap, dt_vel
		- Print key diagnostics at end: T_L at spinup end, T_prod, N_tracer_snaps, N_vel_snaps, U_rms, k_peak

- Step 2:
	* sub-task: Data quality validation and inverse cascade confirmation
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* code_execution_timeout: 600s

	* instructions:
		- Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, diagnostics.npy, energy_spectrum.npy from the data directory
		- Compute mean tracer displacement per snapshot step; wrap Δr to [−π, π] for each component before computing magnitude to handle periodic boundary crossings correctly; verify mean displacement < 0.3 × 2π ≈ 1.88; print result and flag failure if exceeded
		- Compute VACF C_vv(τ) at lags τ = 1, 2, 4, 8, 16 steps using tracer_velocities; verify C_vv(τ=1) > 0.3; report decorrelation time τ_corr as the first lag where C_vv drops below 1/e
		- Plot E(k) at t = 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 1.0 × T_prod overlaid on a single panel with log-log axes; mark k_peak at each time; confirm k_peak drifts from ~4 toward ~1–2
		- Combine E(k) cascade plot and VACF plot into a single multi-panel figure; save as high-resolution PNG
		- Print all key numerical results: mean displacement, C_vv at each lag, τ_corr, k_peak at each time fraction

- Step 3:
	* sub-task: Non-stationarity analysis — compute α(t), γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t) in overlapping windows
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* code_execution_timeout: 1800s

	* instructions:
		- Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, diagnostics.npy from the data directory; load sim_params.json to get T_L
		- Define overlapping windows of width W = 8 T_L, step ΔW = 2 T_L; extract up to ~26 windows
		- For each window: compute displacement increments at lags τ = {0.5, 1, 2} × T_L (not cumulative from t=0); verify isotropy by comparing x and y displacement distributions; pool x and y components; apply McCulloch quantile method to get α per lag; report median α across lags as window estimate and standard deviation across lags as uncertainty
		- For each window: also compute Hill estimator on the pooled displacement magnitudes as cross-check
		- For each window: compute MSD(τ) at multiple lags and fit power law to get γ(t); compute VACF and fit algebraic decay to get ν(t); save the full MSD and VACF curves (not just exponents) for later plotting
		- Extract d_vv(t) and k_peak(t) from diagnostics averaged over each window
		- Run ADF and KPSS stationarity tests on α(t), γ(t), ν(t) time series; print all test statistics and p-values
		- Fit α = a + b × (k_peak/k_box)^c to the α(t) vs k_peak(t)/k_box data; print fit parameters
		- Save all window results including per-window MSD curves, VACF curves, α_McCulloch, α_Hill, γ, ν, d_vv, k_peak, window center times to a single npz file
		- Print all numerical results

- Step 4:
	* sub-task: Wavelet decomposition, eddy-lifetime estimation, and filtered tracer re-advection
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* code_execution_timeout: 7200s

	* instructions:
		- Load velocity_snapshots.npy (shape N_vel_snaps × 2 × 256 × 256), vel_times.npy, tracer_positions.npy, tracer_times.npy from the data directory
		- Apply 2D DWT (Daubechies db4, periodic boundary mode) to each velocity snapshot at levels j = 1 to 6; store detail coefficients at each level
		- Reconstruct filtered velocity fields ũ_j for j_min ∈ {1, 2, 3, 4} by zeroing detail coefficients below j_min; store reconstructed fields
		- For each scale j, compute wavelet energy E_j(x,y,t) = sum of squared detail coefficients across H/V/D subbands; compute temporal autocorrelation C_E(τ,j) averaged over all spatial positions; integrate trapezoidal to get T_eddy(j), stopping when C_E drops below 0.1
		- Re-advect tracer initial positions in each filtered field ũ_j using RK4 with bilinear interpolation between saved velocity snapshots; use Δt_adv = 2.0 (matching Δt_vel) and limit to the first 500 tracer snapshots worth of time to stay within timeout
		- For each filtered field: compute α(j) via McCulloch method on displacement increments, γ_MSD(j) from MSD power law fit, ν_VACF(j) from VACF algebraic decay fit
		- Fit α(j) vs T_eddy(j) to extract functional form; test CTRW prediction α = 1 + β/(2−β) with β ∝ T_eddy^δ by fitting δ
		- Save all wavelet results (T_eddy per scale, α(j), γ(j), ν(j), C_E curves, fit parameters) to npz file
		- Print all numerical results including T_eddy values, α(j), γ(j), ν(j), δ, and CTRW residuals

- Step 5:
	* sub-task: LCS identification via Okubo-Weiss, FTLE computation, residence/flight time analysis
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* code_execution_timeout: 3600s

	* instructions:
		- Load velocity_snapshots.npy, vel_times.npy, tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, diagnostics.npy from the data directory
		- Priority 1: Compute Okubo-Weiss parameter Q = s² − ω² from velocity snapshots using finite differences; use dynamic threshold Q_threshold(t) = σ_Q(t); classify each tracer at each time as vortex-trapped (Q < −Q_threshold), strain-dominated (Q > +Q_threshold), or neutral via bilinear interpolation of Q field at tracer positions
		- Priority 2: Compute α separately for vortex-trapped and strain-dominated tracer populations using McCulloch method on displacement increments; print α values for each population
		- Priority 3: Extract residence time distributions and flight displacement distributions from trajectory segments classified by Q; test for power-law tails via CCDF log-log fit; compare α from CCDF slope to McCulloch α; distinguish vortex-death vs strain-stripping ejection events and compute fractions
		- Priority 4 (if time permits): Compute FTLE on 256×256 grid at exactly 3 representative time snapshots (t = 0.25, 0.5, 0.75 × T_prod) with integration time ΔT_FTLE = 2 × T_L using bilinear interpolation between velocity snapshots
		- Save all LCS/FTLE results to npz file including Q fields, tracer classifications, α per population, residence/flight distributions, ejection fractions, and FTLE fields if computed
		- Print key statistics: α_vortex, α_strain, ejection fractions, power-law fit results

- Step 6:
	* sub-task: Causal link analysis — cross-correlation and Granger causality between spectral condensation and α(t)
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* code_execution_timeout: 600s

	* instructions:
		- Load window results npz from Step 3 (α_McCulloch(t), k_peak(t), window center times) and diagnostics; load energy_spectrum.npy and tracer_times.npy
		- Verify that α(t) and k_peak(t) time series contain at least 15 data points; if fewer than 15 windows are available, skip Granger causality and report only cross-correlation
		- Compute time-lagged cross-correlation between dE(k=1)/dt and dα(t)/dt at lags 0, 1, 2, 3 windows; report peak lag and correlation coefficient
		- If ≥ 15 windows: run Granger causality test using statsmodels grangercausalitytests with max lag = 3 on the (α(t), k_peak(t)) bivariate series; report F-statistics and p-values for each lag
		- Load LCS results from Step 5; compute spatial density of hyperbolic points (Q > +Q_threshold) per time snapshot; interpolate to window centers; compute Pearson correlation between hyperbolic point density and α(t)
		- Save all causal analysis results (cross-correlation arrays, Granger test results, hyperbolic density correlation) to npz/json file
		- Print all test statistics, p-values, and effective degrees of freedom

- Step 7:
	* sub-task: Generate all plots — merged multi-panel figures covering all analysis results
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* code_execution_timeout: 600s

	* instructions:
		- Load all saved results from Steps 2–6 (window results npz, wavelet results npz, LCS results npz, causal results, energy spectra, diagnostics, sim_params.json)
		- Figure 1 (multi-panel): E(k) cascade at 4 time fractions + VACF C_vv(τ) vs lag; mark k_peak drift and τ_corr
		- Figure 2 (multi-panel): α(t) with McCulloch and Hill overlaid, γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t) vs window center time; annotate ADF/KPSS stationarity results as text on panels
		- Figure 3 (multi-panel): α(t) vs k_peak(t)/k_box scatter with fitted curve α = a + b × (k_peak/k_box)^c; representative MSD curves for 3 selected windows; representative VACF curves for 3 selected windows
		- Figure 4 (multi-panel): α(j) vs T_eddy(j) with CTRW prediction curve; γ(j) vs T_eddy(j); ν(j) vs T_eddy(j); wavelet energy autocorrelation C_E(τ,j) for each scale
		- Figure 5 (multi-panel): LCS classification + FTLE field snapshot at representative time with tracer overlay; vortex-trapped vs strain-dominated α comparison bar chart; residence time CCDF and flight displacement CCDF log-log plots
		- Figure 6 (multi-panel): Cross-correlation between dE(k=1)/dt and dα/dt vs lag; Granger causality p-values vs lag; 2D map of α as function of (T_eddy, k_peak/k_box)
		- Save all 6 figures as high-resolution PNG files (dpi=300); print file paths

- Step 8:
	* sub-task: Write full Results section of the paper
	* agent in charge: researcher

	* instructions:
		- Synthesize all numerical results from Steps 2–7 into a complete, publication-quality Results section with subsections matching the four analysis tasks
		- Subsection 1 (Data Quality and Inverse Cascade): report mean displacement, C_vv(τ=1), τ_corr, k_peak drift from ~4 to ~1–2 over T_prod
		- Subsection 2 (Non-Stationarity Analysis): report α(t) time series with McCulloch and Hill estimates, γ(t), ν(t), ADF/KPSS test results, fit parameters for α = a + b × (k_peak/k_box)^c; state whether α is stationary or drifts; compare to prior v1 estimate of α ≈ 1.4
		- Subsection 3 (Wavelet Eddy-Lifetime Scaling): report T_eddy(j) for each scale, α(j), γ(j), ν(j); report functional form α = f(T_eddy); test CTRW prediction α = 1 + β/(2−β) with β ∝ T_eddy^δ; quantify residual between CTRW prediction and measured α(j) and discuss whether deviation is within estimator uncertainty or indicates genuine CTRW breakdown
		- Subsection 4 (LCS and Ejection Dynamics): report α_vortex vs α_strain, ejection fractions (vortex-death vs strain-stripping), power-law exponents from residence time and flight displacement CCDFs, FTLE ridge structure
		- Subsection 5 (Causal Analysis): report cross-correlation peak lag and coefficient, Granger causality F-statistics and p-values, hyperbolic point density correlation with α(t); state whether spectral condensation Granger-causes changes in α
		- Reference all figures by number; include all key quantitative values with uncertainties; write in third-person academic style

name: plan_recorder
content: Plan recorded as final. Planning stage complete.
